Evaluating DALL\u00b7E 3, Midjourney and DreamStudio Beta, we generated five images on each platform for eight common dermatological conditions designated by the American Academy of Dermatology. The Massey\u2013Martin Skin Color Scale was used to evaluate the images, and interrater reliability was further assessed by a nonrater. Sex determination was based on identifying features. We generated 120 images: 88 (73%) had concordant skin tone ratings and 109 (91%) displayed an identifiable sex. Of the 88 images, 85 (97%) were rated light-toned, 3 (3%) were rated medium-toned and 0 were rated dark-toned. Of the 109 images, 74 (68%) were male and 35 (32%) were female. Highlighting substantial biases currently present in common AI platforms, this study underscores the need for AI algorithms to address both skin tone and sex biases as they continue to skyrocket in
